meaning: adj.一元的,单一的,归一的,整体的,[数]单式的
examples: It shouldn't choose the unitary government-driving way of citifying.
In this sense, it's not very unitary.
By keeping quantum states as unit vectors, the transformations are all unitary.
Plymouth and Torbay Councils are not included in the report as they are unitary authorities.
Shropshire Council is facing its first all-out elections since the unitary authority was created in 2009.
